metadata.summaryPolicy: Most summaries are derived from data_prep source table content. Records with summarySource data_prep_and_original_page include only facts visible on checked original pages.

signals.title: Venezuela earthquakes leave people and animals in desperate need
signals.cardSummary: After earthquakes in Venezuela, IFAW discussed urgent deployment with local dog-rescue partners, including emergency veterinary care, animal food, and basic supplies.
signals.detailedSummary: After earthquakes in Venezuela, IFAW discussed urgent deployment with local dog-rescue partners, including emergency veterinary care, animal food, and basic supplies. Potential project direction: Support local partners to run post-disaster animal aid points with veterinary rounds, food, water, crates, and lost-animal registration.
signals.keyInformationSummary: After earthquakes in Venezuela, IFAW discussed urgent deployment with local dog-rescue partners, including emergency veterinary care, animal food, and basic supplies.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support local partners to run post-disaster animal aid points with veterinary rounds, food, water, crates, and lost-animal registration.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns animal welfare, veterinary care, emergency veterinary care, dogs in Venezuela, matching its focus on animal welfare, veterinary care, livestock, stray animals, wildlife, and disaster response.
signals.originalTitle: Venezuela earthquakes leave people and animals in desperate need

signals.title: Firefighters rescue dog from Venezuela earthquake rubble
signals.cardSummary: A dog was rescued from earthquake rubble, showing that animals are often found during human search-and-rescue but lack a systematic response pathway.
signals.detailedSummary: A dog was rescued from earthquake rubble, showing that animals are often found during human search-and-rescue but lack a systematic response pathway. Potential project direction: Fund joint training for human rescue teams and animal responders so animal rescue is included in disaster-response standard operating procedures.

signals.title: Humane World for Animals India helped 12,000 animals after Odisha floods
signals.cardSummary: Humane World for Animals India reported emergency aid for 12,000 animals after floods in Odisha, including more than 36,000 kg of feed for goats, cows, and other animals.
signals.detailedSummary: The original page confirms that floods affected people and animals across Balasore and neighboring districts in Odisha. Humane World for Animals India, with Yuva Vikas Foundation, provided over 36,000 kg of emergency feed to reach 12,000 animals in the worst-affected blocks of Baliapal and Bhograi. The page also notes illness risks such as diarrhea, skin infections, and pox in animals kept in unsanitary and overcrowded flood shelters.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Humane World for Animals India provided emergency aid after Odisha floods, reaching 12,000 animals and distributing more than 36,000 kg of feed for goats, cows, and other animals.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Develop a flood-aftercare project for ruminants focused on feed reserves, distribution, and nutritional recovery.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns animal-inclusive disaster response, emergency feed, livestock welfare, animal health risks, and local partner coordination after flooding.
signals.originalTitle: Humane World for Animals India helped 12,000 animals after Odisha floods
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page was accessible but no clear publication-date field was found. Kept plausible existing date value.

signals.title: Nowzad launches emergency Ukraine animal evacuation
signals.cardSummary: Nowzad launched an emergency Ukraine evacuation to move vulnerable animals away from the frontline, with a GBP 50,000 fund for transport and temporary kennelling.
signals.detailedSummary: The original Vet Times page says Nowzad launched Operation Kramatorsk to evacuate endangered animals from Kramatorsk, less than five miles from the advancing front line, to Smila in central Ukraine. The charity planned repeated rescue trips, was vaccinating animals against rabies, and launched a GBP 50,000 emergency fund for evacuation trips and temporary kennelling.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Nowzad launched an emergency operation to evacuate vulnerable animals from Kramatorsk to Smila in Ukraine and sought GBP 50,000 to sustain the rescue and temporary kennelling work.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support animal evacuation from conflict areas, inter-regional transport, vaccination, microchipping, and temporary housing.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns wartime animal evacuation, veterinary risk, rabies prevention, shelter capacity, and emergency animal protection.
signals.originalTitle: Nowzad launches emergency Ukraine animal evacuation
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page was accessible but no clear publication-date field was found. Kept plausible existing date value.

signals.title: Victoria bushfire and heatwave wildlife response
signals.cardSummary: Humane World for Animals Australia deployed to Victoria after bushfires and heat stress affected wildlife carers, flying foxes, wombats, and burned kangaroos.
signals.detailedSummary: The original page confirms that Humane World for Animals Australia deployed to Victoria after heatwaves and bushfires. It supported Red Box Wildlife Shelter and Animal Abbey with animals suffering heat stress and burns, including flying foxes, wombats, and kangaroos needing rescue, transport, bottle feeding, wound care, and dehydration treatment.

signals.title: Karoo Donkey Sanctuary drought crisis
signals.cardSummary: Karoo Donkey Sanctuary reported a drought-driven welfare crisis affecting rescued donkeys, horses, and farm animals dependent on brought-in feed and water.
signals.detailedSummary: The original IOL page says drought in South Africa has endangered animals at Karoo Donkey Sanctuary. The sanctuary reported responsibility for 138 rescued donkeys, 38 rescued horses, and more than 250 rescued farm animals, with no grazing left, dam levels below 10%, and daily feed and water purchases needed for survival.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Karoo Donkey Sanctuary reported that drought endangered 138 rescued donkeys, 38 rescued horses, and more than 250 rescued farm animals, creating urgent feed and water needs.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Fund emergency feed, water, veterinary care, and a long-term drought-reserve plan for donkey sanctuaries.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns drought-related animal welfare, rescued equines and farm animals, emergency feed, water access, and sanctuary support.
signals.originalTitle: Karoo Donkey Sanctuary drought crisis
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page date field found via JSON-LD datePublished: 10 Feb 2026.

signals.title: FAO global avian influenza update reports recent animal outbreaks/events
signals.cardSummary: FAO reported a global avian influenza situation update covering zoonotic-potential viruses, with hundreds of officially reported HPAI animal outbreaks since the previous update.
signals.detailedSummary: The original FAO page is a 28 May 2026 situation update on avian influenza viruses with zoonotic potential. It says 763 HPAI outbreaks or events in animals were officially reported in 31 countries or territories since the previous update, caused mostly by H5N1, and notes five officially reported human events in the same period.
signals.keyInformationSummary: FAO reported 763 officially reported HPAI animal outbreaks or events in 31 countries or territories since the previous update, mostly involving H5N1.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support biosecurity training for small poultry holders, wild-bird contact risk education, and veterinary monitoring.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns transboundary animal disease surveillance, farm animal welfare, zoonotic risk, and veterinary preparedness.
signals.originalTitle: FAO global avian influenza update reports recent animal outbreaks/events
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page was accessible but no clear publication-date field was found. Kept plausible existing date value.

signals.title: Spain has many pets but high abandonment remains
signals.cardSummary: Euro Weekly News reported that Spain has more than 15 million registered domestic animals while shelters and rescue services continue to face high dog and cat intake.
signals.detailedSummary: The original article cites Spanish government data showing more than 15 million domestic animals in households, including about 7.56 million dogs and 5.62 million cats. It also reports that animal welfare estimates and Fundacion Affinity figures indicate around 292,000 dogs and cats were taken into shelters or collected by rescue services in 2024, creating persistent pressure on shelters.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Spain has more than 15 million registered domestic animals, while around 292,000 dogs and cats were reportedly taken into shelters or collected by rescue services in 2024.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support adoption education, abandonment prevention, low-cost sterilisation, and pet registration.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns companion animal abandonment, shelter pressure, policy implementation, and dog and cat welfare in Europe.
signals.originalTitle: Spain has many pets but high abandonment remains
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page date field found via meta article:published_time: 6 Jun 2026.

signals.title: PDSA warns of “petflation” health crisis
signals.cardSummary: PDSA warned of a pet health crisis linked to rising care costs, with some owners delaying veterinary care, going into debt, or cutting essentials to pay pet bills.
signals.detailedSummary: The original Vet Times page reports PDSA concerns about pet health inequality and rising veterinary costs. PDSA said it provided care to more than 420,000 animals last year and cited survey findings that some owners delayed vet visits, went into debt, or cut back on essentials to afford pet care.
signals.keyInformationSummary: PDSA warned that rising veterinary costs and cost-of-living pressures are creating pet health risks, including delayed care and debt among owners.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support basic veterinary vouchers for low-income households, covering vaccination, deworming, and sterilisation.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns access to veterinary care, owner affordability, and companion animal health systems.
signals.originalTitle: PDSA warns of “petflation” health crisis

signals.title: 47 cats rescued after shelter roof collapse in storm
signals.cardSummary: Safe Haven Pet Sanctuary evacuated 47 cats after a strong storm caused a partial roof collapse and flooding at its Green Bay building.
signals.detailedSummary: The original WBAY page reports that Safe Haven Pet Sanctuary was condemned after a strong storm partially collapsed the roof and flooded the first floor. Forty-seven cats were rescued, no injuries were reported, and the cats were moved to other shelters, foster homes, Animal House, Oak View Veterinary Hospital, or Safe Haven temporary arrangements.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Forty-seven cats were rescued from Safe Haven Pet Sanctuary after a storm caused roof collapse and flooding in Green Bay, Wisconsin.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support small-shelter building-risk assessment, emergency relocation planning, and repair or disaster-insurance funds.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns shelter disaster preparedness, cat rescue, emergency relocation, foster capacity, and veterinary support after storm damage.

signals.title: Vietnam police rescue more than 400 cats from meat crime ring
signals.cardSummary: Police in Vietnam rescued more than 400 cats in a cat meat crime ring case in Ho Chi Minh City, with animal welfare groups involved in care for survivors.
signals.detailedSummary: The original Guardian page reports that police rescued more than 400 cats in Ho Chi Minh City after investigating pet thefts linked to a cat meat crime ring. Officers found live cats in cages, some cats were reunited with owners, several dozen died due to harsh conditions, and animal welfare groups helped care for survivors.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Vietnam police rescued more than 400 cats in Ho Chi Minh City in a case linked to a cat meat crime ring and pet thefts.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support anti-pet-theft work, cat-and-dog meat trade rescue, quarantine, rehabilitation, and public education.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns companion animal trafficking, cat meat trade enforcement, rescue care, and animal welfare policy.
signals.originalTitle: Vietnam police rescue more than 400 cats from meat crime ring
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page date field found via meta article:published_time: 17 Jun 2026.

signals.title: Spain arrests suspects in exotic animal trafficking network
signals.cardSummary: Spanish authorities arrested two people and investigated eight others in an exotic animal trafficking case involving protected animals, dogs, and cats.
signals.detailedSummary: The original Asharq Al Awsat page reports that Spanish Guardia Civil investigated suspected wildlife crimes in Seville, intercepted protected animals allegedly transported without required documentation, and seized 256 animals during searches. Officers also found 61 dogs and 28 cats believed to be bred for illegal sale.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Spanish authorities arrested two people and seized 256 animals in an exotic animal trafficking investigation; officers also found 61 dogs and 28 cats believed to be bred for illegal sale.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support temporary holding, veterinary checks, and welfare training for law-enforcement officers handling seized wildlife.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns wildlife trafficking, exotic pet trade enforcement, illegal breeding, and companion animal welfare.
signals.originalTitle: Spain arrests suspects in exotic animal trafficking network
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page date field found via meta og:updated_time: 20 Jun 2026.

signals.title: Asia-Pacific customs agencies unite against wildlife trafficking
signals.cardSummary: IFAW reported that customs and law enforcement agencies across Asia-Pacific reaffirmed cooperation against transnational wildlife trafficking networks.
signals.detailedSummary: The original IFAW page reports that agencies from across Asia-Pacific met in Chengdu for Operation Mekong Dragon VIII, supported by IFAW, TRAFFIC, WWF, China Customs, UNODC, and WCO RILO-AP. It says more than 60 representatives from over 20 countries and regions attended, and that Operation Mekong Dragon has made more than 6,626 seizures since 2018, including more than 630 tons of endangered species and products.
signals.keyInformationSummary: IFAW reported an Asia-Pacific law enforcement meeting under Operation Mekong Dragon VIII to strengthen cooperation against wildlife trafficking networks.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Support rescue procedures for seized live animals, quarantine, rehabilitation, and cross-border transport standards.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Welttierschutzgesellschaft because the source concerns wildlife crime, cross-border enforcement, institutional cooperation, and protection of trafficked animals.
signals.originalTitle: Asia-Pacific customs agencies unite against wildlife trafficking
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page date field found via JSON-LD datePublished: 12 Jun 2026.
